subject: PhD Logic
instructions: Complete seven problems, including one from each section. Problems 1–3 concern General logic; Problems 4–6 concern Model theory; Problems 7–9 concern Set theory; Problems 10–12 concern Computability.

problems.text: State the incompleteness theorem and explain the role of Goedel numbering in its proof.
Sketch the proof of the compactness theorem for first order logic.
Sketch the proof of Tarski’s undefinability of truth.
Prove that the theory of dense linear orders without endpoints is complete.
Find two models which are elementarily equivalent but not isomorphic.
State and prove Tarski’s criterion for elementary submodels.
Sketch the proof of \(L\) satisfying CH.
State and prove the infinite Ramsey theorem.
Prove that every stationary subset of omega one can be split into aleph one many pairwise disjoint stationary subsets.
Show how to construct a simple recursively enumerable set \(A\) (meaning that \(\omega-A\) has no infinite r.e. subset).
Define the recursively enumerable set \(K=\{a:\{a\}(a)\mathord\downarrow\}\). Use the \(S_1^1\) function to show that \(K\) is many-one complete.
Prove that the following are equivalent, for any nonempty \(A\subset\omega\):

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: \(A\) is the domain of some partial recursive function \(\phi\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: \(A\) is the range of some (total) recursive function \(f\).
